name: mutmut (Python Mutation Testing)

performance.notes: Runs test suite once per mutation; can take hours on large codebases

agent_readiness.notes: Mutation testing for Python — measures test suite quality. mutmut run. mutmut results. mutmut show <id> (diff for surviving mutant). Mutates: arithmetic operators, comparisons, string/bool constants, return values. Surviving mutants = untested code paths. CI: mutmut run && mutmut junitxml > results.xml. --paths-to-mutate to limit scope. Pairs with pytest. Reveals test gaps that coverage alone misses.

gotchas: Very slow — each mutation re-runs full test suite
Large codebases require --paths-to-mutate to keep runtime manageable
High surviving mutant count can be overwhelming to fix
Not all surviving mutants represent real test gaps (equivalent mutants)
best_when: Measuring test suite quality beyond code coverage
Critical business logic that needs comprehensive test coverage
Before shipping security-critical or financial code
avoid_when: Large codebases without scope limiting (runtime explosion)
Projects with slow test suites (multiplied by mutation count)
When 80% line coverage already meets project standards
summary: mutmut runs mutation testing for Python — systematically breaks code and checks if tests catch it. Surviving mutants reveal untested code paths that coverage misses. mutmut run → mutmut results → mutmut show <id>. Very slow (runs tests per mutation). Use --paths-to-mutate for scoping. Best for critical business logic where test quality matters most.
